bullets: Lead 20+ staff across R&D and global sales in Seoul and the US, driving AI-powered radiation therapy solutions that captured 25% domestic market share and ranked #1 in clinical performance benchmarks by leading Japanese research institutions.
Spearhead international expansion across the US, Latin America, Southeast Asia, and the Middle East, securing 10+ distribution partnerships and targeting 100% year-over-year revenue growth.
Led a 10+ member R&D team and established a structured product development lifecycle, reducing release cycles from ~2 months to 2–3 weeks through overlapping, parallelized workflows.
Elevated AI model accuracy and reliability by enforcing data integrity standards and advancing model architecture, culminating in MFDS (Korean FDA) clinical trial approval.
Founded and scaled the AI department from zero to 15 members, leading the development of an AI-powered cloud platform for 3D bioprinted wound regeneration targeting diabetic foot ulcers (DFU).
Drove the full commercial cycle from MVP to $1B+ in long-term contracts across the US, India, Turkey, the Middle East, and Latin America, achieving both FDA and CE clearance for the solution.

Launched the AI cloud service on AWS in 6 months, then migrated to Google Cloud under a strategic partnership, establishing production-grade MLOps infrastructure in 3 months to support global commercial deployment.
Led and completed 4-year AR/MR research projects for Samsung Research, securing grants and publishing top-tier conference papers (UIST, CHI).
Developed Android and web applications for a social donation campaign platform.
Designed interfaces for mobile, tablet, TV, and smartwatch, developing prototypes for proof-of-concept across convergence scenarios.
Created a mobile 3D interface using sensors and image processing; resulting work was recognized with a Samsung Best Paper Award and a granted US patent.
